explanation: à — used before city names for location or residence: à Paris, à Lyon, à Rome. Rule: à + ville.

quizQuestion:
Viens {___} la maison !
(Come to the house!)
explanation: à — marks destination or location: à la maison (at home). Contracts with le: à + le = au; with les: à + les = aux.

quizQuestion:
Je vis {___} ville.
(I live in the city.)
explanation: en — used in fixed location expressions (en ville, en classe) and before feminine country names. No article follows: en ville, not en la ville.

quizQuestion:
Elle habite {___} France.
(She lives in France.)
explanation: en — used before feminine country names (most ending in -e): en France, en Espagne, en Italie. Compare: au + masculine country (au Japon, au Canada).

quizQuestion:
Le chat est {___} la boîte.
(The cat is inside the box.)
sentenceTranslation: The cat is inside the box.
explanation: dans — means inside a defined, enclosed space: dans la boîte, dans la salle, dans le tiroir. Always followed by a noun with an article.

quizQuestion:
Posez-le {___} la table.
(Lay it on the table.)
grammarPointLabel: sur — Preposition (on top of a surface)
explanation: sur — means on or on top of a surface: sur la table, sur le lit, sur le bureau. Contrast with sous (under).

quizQuestion:
Le chien dort {___} la chaise.
(The dog is sleeping under the chair.)
sentenceTranslation: The dog is sleeping under the chair.
explanation: sous — means under or beneath: sous la chaise, sous le lit, sous la table. Contrast with sur (on top of).

quizQuestion:
Je t'attends {___} l'école.
(I'm waiting for you in front of the school.)
sentenceTranslation: I'm waiting for you in front of the school.
explanation: devant — means in front of: devant l'école, devant la porte, devant la maison. Contrast with derrière (behind).

quizQuestion:
Le jardin est {___} la maison.
(The garden is behind the house.)
sentenceTranslation: The garden is behind the house.
explanation: derrière — means behind: derrière la maison, derrière l'école. Contrast with devant (in front of).

quizQuestion:
J'étudie l'anglais {___} moi.
(I study English at home.)
explanation: chez — means at the home or place of someone: chez moi (at my place), chez Marie, chez le médecin (at the doctor's). Always followed by a person or tonic pronoun, never a place noun.

quizQuestion:
On se retrouve {___} Marc ce soir.
(We're meeting at Marc's place tonight.)
sentenceTranslation: We're meeting at Marc's place tonight.
grammarPointLabel: chez — Preposition (at a named person's place)
explanation: chez — used with proper names and pronouns, never with place nouns: chez Marc, chez elle. Contrast: à la bibliothèque (place noun requires à + article).

quizQuestion:
Je viens {___} mes amis.
(I'm coming with my friends.)
grammarPointLabel: avec — Preposition (with / in the company of)
explanation: avec — means with (accompaniment or manner): avec mes amis, avec plaisir. Opposite: sans (without).

quizQuestion:
Ce cadeau est {___} toi.
(This gift is for you.)
explanation: pour — marks purpose, destination, or beneficiary: pour toi, pour le travail, partir pour Paris. Use pour when something is intended for someone or aimed at a goal.

quizQuestion:
— Qui est-ce ? — {___} mon professeur.
(— Who is it? — It's my teacher.)
sentenceTranslation: — Who is it? — It's my teacher.
explanation: c'est — for identifying a person when followed by any determiner (possessive, article): c'est mon professeur, c'est une amie. When introducing who someone is, always c'est (not il/elle est) before a noun with a determiner.

quizQuestion:
{___} enseignant.
(He is a teacher. [describing profession without article])
grammarPointLabel: il est — Description (il est + bare profession/adjective)
explanation: il est / elle est — used before a bare adjective or unmodified profession noun (no article): il est enseignant, elle est médecin, il est grand. Contrast: c'est un enseignant (with article = identification).
